LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UNITED FOOTBALL LEAGUE LLC AND UFL TEAM SAN FRANCISCO LLC'S PETITION TO STAY THE ACTION AND COMPEL ARBITRATION IS OFF CALENDAR. AMENDED MOTION FILED AND TO BE HEARD ON FEB. 24, 2012. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UNITED FOOTBALL LEAGUE, LLC AND UFL TEAM SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S PETITION TO STAY THE ACTION AND COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE UNDERLYING DISPUTE IS GOVERNED BY THE ARBITRATION PROVISION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY PLAINTIFF GREEN, DENNIS
OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S MOTION TO LIFE THE STAY FILED BY DEFENDANT UNITED FOOTBALL LEAGUE LLC UFL TEAM SAN FRANCISCO LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE WAS A PARTNER AT MORRISON & FOERSTER FROM 1997 THROUGH 2/7/2005 PRIOR TO HER APPOINTMENT TO THE BENCH. THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF MR. GREEN WAS SERVED BY THE MORRISON & FOERSTER LAW FIRM IN NEW YORK. SHE DOES NOT HAVE ANY DOUBT TO BE FAIR & IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. PLAINTIFF DENNIS GREEN'S MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY AND DECLARE THE ARBITRATION PROVISION UNCONSCIONABLE, OR, IN THE ALTERANTIVE, TO DESIGNATE AN IMPARTIAL ARBITRATOR AND ORDER THAT DEFENDANTS PAY THE FULL ARBITRATION COSTS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS NO BASIS TO LIFT THE STAY OR ORDER A NEW ARBITRATOR AT THIS POINT BECAUSE THERE ARE NO NEW FACTS OR LEGAL BASIS TO DO SO. THERE WILL ALSO BE NO JUDICIAL ECONNOMY SERVED BY LIFTING THE STAY AND ALSO ANOTHER JUDGE HAS RULED IN GRANTING THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE ARGUMENT REGARDING ARBITRATION FEES BEING EXCESSIVE IS WITHOUT MERIT OR THERE IS NO SHOWING BY MR. GREEN THAT THIS FEES ARE EXPENSIVE FOR HIM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Continued To Jul-16-14 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dan Siegel, Esq. of Seigel & Yee (510-839-1200) for Judgment Creditor, Dennis Green.
Paul Pelosi, UFL ownership team member (415-788-8600), Judgment Debtor
Dennis Miller of Stein & Lubin LLP (415-981-0550) specially appearing for William Hambrecht, Judgment Debtor.
William Humbrecht, Judgment Debtor is NOT present.
Court Reporter: Krishanna Derita, CSR#11945, (925-360-6891) Reported.
Judge: Harry Dorfman; Clerk: M. Goodman. Reported. (514)

2:20 pm
The Court is informed of the status of the case. Mr. Seigel raises the issue of Judgment Debtor's absence. Judgment Debtor William Hambrecht is out of the country per report of Mr. Miller. The Court accepts good faith representation that Mr. Hambrecht is to appear in the next hearing. Mr. Pelosi, informs the Court that he will answer question from the Judgment Creditor.

2:50pm
The Court orders the following: The Order of Examination is continued to July 16, 2014 (Wednesday) at 2:00 pm for further examination. Mr. Pelosi is ordered present on July 16, 2014.The Court orders that a Bench letter be issued as to Judgment Debtor William Hambrecht for failure to appear on Order of examination today. The Bench letter is stayed till July 16, 2014 at 2:00pm.

Court is adjourned.

Notice Of Motion To Life The Stay And Declare The Arbitration Provision Unconscionable, Or, In The Alterantive, To Designate An Impartial Arbitrator And Order That Defts Pay The Full Arbitration Costs
Off Calendar Jan-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY, DAN SIEGEL OF SIEGEL & YEE, TEL. NO. (510) 839-1200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KHARI J. TIELLERY OF KEKER & VAN NEST LLP, TEL. NO. 773-6621 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE WAS A PARTNER AT MORRISON & FOERSTER FROM 1997 THROUGH 2/7/2005 PRIOR TO HER APPOINTMENT TO THE BENCH. THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF MR. GREEN WAS SERVED BY THE MORRISON & FOERSTER LAW FIRM IN NEW YORK. SHE DOES NOT HAVE ANY DOUBT TO BE FAIR & IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF DENNIS GREEN's Motion To Lift The Stay And Declare The Arbitration Provision Unconscionable, Or, In The Alterantive, To Designate An Impartial Arbitrator And Order That Defendants Pay The Full Arbitration Costs IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS NO BASIS TO LIFT THE STAY OR ORDER A NEW ARBITRATOR AT THIS POINT BECAUSE THERE ARE NO NEW FACTS OR LEGAL BASIS TO DO SO. THERE WILL ALSO BE NO JUDICIAL ECONNOMY SERVED BY LIFTING THE STAY AND ALSO ANOTHER JUDGE HAS RULED IN GRANTING THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE ARGUMENT REGARDING ARBITRATION FEES BEING EXCESSIVE IS WITHOUT MERIT OR THERE IS NO SHOWING BY MR. GREEN THAT THIS FEES ARE EXPENSIVE FOR HIM. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (302/MJM)
